title: Calibrate confidence on the vet-sizing assumptions
guidance: Reward calibration that varies with the strength of the evidence and reads cleanly as a table. Penalise blanket confidence or uncalibrated ratings.

runner_input.instruction: Rate the confidence of each claim/assumption below and give a one-line rationale.
runner_input.constraints: Output: a markdown table with exactly these columns: Claim | Confidence | Rationale.

items: Assumption A: ~70% software penetration. Evidence: RCVS practice data (strong).
Assumption B: £51k average ACV. Evidence: comparable products £45–55k (medium).
Assumption C: 9% growth sustains 5 years. Evidence: extrapolated from 2 years (thin).
Assumption D: incumbents won't respond. Evidence: they hold the base but lack the product (contested).
